title: The Israel-Lebanon Recipe for Never-Ending War
gist: Ben-Ami argues the new US-brokered Israel-Lebanon framework is dead on arrival because it conditions Israel's withdrawal on Hezbollah's 'verified disarmament'—an outcome made unlikely by Lebanese politics and Iran's strengthened position—while doing nothing to sever Hezbollah's Iranian lifeline.

insights.claim: Lebanon's political circumstances combined with Iran's strengthened regional position make Hezbollah's complete disarmament highly unlikely.
insights.speaker: SHLOMO BEN-AMI
insights.so_what: If disarmament is the withdrawal precondition and it won't happen, expect an indefinite Israeli presence and persistent conflict risk in southern Lebanon.
insights.quote: Lebanon’s political circumstances, together with Iran’s strengthened regional position, make Hezbollah’s complete disarmament highly unlikely.

insights.claim: The framework agreement is dead on arrival because it conditions Israel's withdrawal on Hezbollah's disarmament but contains no provisions to cut Hezbollah's Iranian lifeline.
insights.speaker: SHLOMO BEN-AMI
insights.so_what: Structural design flaw signals continued instability; discount any near-term de-escalation premium in regional risk pricing.
insights.quote: the new framework agreement is dead on arrival

insights.claim: Late in June 2026, Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement with the United States that Lebanon's chief negotiator Nada Hamadeh Moawad called a first step toward restoring Lebanese sovereignty.
insights.speaker: SHLOMO BEN-AMI
insights.so_what: Establishes the diplomatic baseline; the gap between official optimism and the author's DOA verdict frames the credibility risk.

insights.claim: The departure of the Israel Defense Forces from southern Lebanon hinges explicitly on the 'verified disarmament' of Iran's proxy Hezbollah.
insights.speaker: SHLOMO BEN-AMI
insights.so_what: The withdrawal trigger is a verification standard unlikely to be met, implying prolonged occupation—watch for verification-mechanism disputes.
insights.quote: hinges on the “verified disarmament” of Iran’s proxy, Hezbollah
